headline: Thomas Piketty: 'In the face of Trump's militaristic drift and looming disaster, Europe must equip itself to have global influence'
subhead: Following the intervention in Venezuela, the war against Iran launched by the US president signals yet another admission of weakness by the United States, observes the economist in his column. This military agitation primarily serves to bolster the US economy.

headline: In Beirut, Israeli bombs unleash chaos
subhead: Israel's ground offensive has triggered mass displacement in predominantly Shiite and Christian neighborhoods in the southern part of the city.

headline: US State Department approves 'emergency' weapons sale to Israel
subhead: While US arms sales typically require approval by Congress, the secretary of state issued a waiver bypassing the approval. Major US defense companies have agreed to quadruple production of advanced weapons in an effort to 'improve Israel's capability to meet current and future threats.'

headline: Footage from the Strait of Hormuz reveals how the war in Iran has disrupted global maritime traffic
subhead: Maritime traffic data, video footage and satellite images document how oil and gas infrastructure, along with several ships, have been affected by the war involving Israel, the United States and Iran.

headline: Middle Eastern countries suffer vastly different death tolls as the conflict spreads from Iran
subhead: More than 1,000 people have been killed in the region since February 28, when the US and Israel started a war against Iran, with the majority of casualties being reported in Iran and Lebanon.

Iranian school hit by air strike: Le Monde's investigation confirms many civilian victims, including children
subhead: Videos obtained and analyzed by Le Monde confirmed that the bombing of a school in Minab, Iran, on the first day of joint US-Israeli strikes on the country, caused many civilian victims, including children.
byline: Alexandre Horn,  Asia Balluffier and  Ghazal Golshiri

headline: War in Iran puts political pressure on both isolationist Vance and interventionist Rubio
subhead: Neither the US vice president nor the secretary of state stands to gain from a show of military force that lacks a clear strategy and runs counter to the MAGA doctrine. The conflict is primarily Donald Trump's, who appears unconcerned about his political legacy.

headline: Azerbaijan and Armenia, both neighbors of Iran, fear regional spillover of conflict
subhead: Two Iranian drones struck Azerbaijan's Nakhchivan region on Thursday, raising fears of an expansion of hostilities in the South Caucasus, as Baku maintains close ties with Israel.
